meta.name: Semantix
meta.scratchpad: Semantix Spec Studio tracker initialized. Claude should execute one bounded task at a time in dependency order and Codex will review with targeted tests.

swimlanes.label: Contract
swimlanes.description: Semantix-side packet contracts, fixtures, and validator parity.

swimlanes.label: Evaluator
swimlanes.description: Alignment evaluation, readiness, stable IDs, and user-turn semantics.

swimlanes.label: Integration
swimlanes.description: Phalanx-facing handshake, context requests, and degraded behavior.

swimlanes.label: Proof
swimlanes.description: End-to-end fixtures, reports, and review handoff evidence.

tasks.title: Implement Semantix Spec Studio packet contract module
tasks.goal: Add Semantix-side typed contract definitions and validation helpers for the Phalanx Spec Studio alignment packet, context request, context response, and degraded packet shapes.
definition_of_done: Repo-native named exports exist for SemantixAlignmentPacket, SemantixContextRequest, SemantixContextResponse, readiness values ready/needs_user/blocked, existingSystemContext modes new/update/unknown, contextSources, groundedFacts, findings, and nextTurn.

Validation rejects malformed readiness, missing existingSystemContext, ready+unknown mode, ready update without targetSurfaces, ready update without non-change/reuse/compatibility boundaries, grounded facts without evidenceRef, and Staff-owned output fields.
Validation accepts the upstream sample packet semantics for greenfield ready, update ready, ambiguous needs_user, duplicate/replacement blocked, Hoplon-grounded update, and degraded packets.
No Phalanx runtime code is modified from the Semantix repo.
constraints: Do not treat Semantix as Staff or planner authority.
Do not emit Staff design docs, feature puzzles, verify commands, implementation plans, or file-change instructions from the packet.
Keep groundedFacts evidence-backed and separate from assumptions, risks, recommendations, findings, and requirements.
Use targeted tests only.
tasks.comment: Landed packages/stx/src/spec-studio-contracts.js: validators + asserters for SemantixAlignmentPacket / SemantixContextRequest / SemantixContextResponse / contextSources / groundedFacts / findings / nextTurn, plus READINESS, EXISTING_SYSTEM_MODE, FINDING_*, CONTEXT_SOURCE_*, CONTEXT_REQUEST enums, STAFF_OWNED_FIELDS. 6 upstream samples transcribed in tests/fixtures/spec-studio-samples.js. 63 tests pass; full repo green.

context.claude_brief: Read docs/phalanx-spec-studio-integration-contract.md and implement only the Semantix-owned packet contract surface. Keep exports named and add narrow tests.
suggested_tests: npm run test --workspace @semantix/stx -- --runInBand or an equivalent targeted package test command if available

tasks.title: Define Semantix evaluation request and response interface
tasks.goal: Create the Semantix-side evaluation seam Phalanx can call or simulate: raw directive plus prior packet/session state in, alignment packet plus events/contextRequests out.
definition_of_done: SemantixEvaluateRequest supports trigger initial/user_turn/reconsider/context_response/decide_all/skip.
Every non-initial request requires currentPacket, decisions, findings, and contextResponses arrays so stable IDs can be preserved.
SemantixEvaluateResponse returns packet, events, and contextRequests.
The seam can be called in tests without starting a long-running Semantix service.
constraints: Do not hardcode a Project-Phalanx repo path as the only integration mode.
Do not query Hoplon directly from Semantix code.
Do not create a live server in this slice unless explicitly approved.
tasks.comment: Landed packages/stx/src/spec-studio-evaluator.js: EVALUATE_TRIGGER constants for initial/user_turn/reconsider/context_response/decide_all/skip, validateSemantixEvaluateRequest enforcing currentPacket on non-initial triggers and array shape of decisions/findings/contextResponses, validateSemantixEvaluateResponse covering packet+events+contextRequests, and createSemantixEvaluator(impl) seam runnable in tests without a server. 35 targeted tests pass; full repo 123 + 27 green.

context.claude_brief: Implement the evaluator interface as a dormant adapter/helper with tests. Do not start services or wire runtime launch paths.

tasks.title: Preserve stable IDs across Spec Studio turns
tasks.goal: Make Semantix preserve requirement, finding, decision, grounded-fact, and context-request IDs across multi-turn evaluation when Phalanx passes prior session state.
definition_of_done: A follow-up evaluation carries forward unchanged requirement and finding IDs.
Changed requirements/findings are represented through supersession rather than silent same-ID mutation.
Resolved findings remain auditable and are not silently removed.
Tests cover preserved IDs, superseded IDs, dropped IDs rejected/reported, and same-ID mutation rejected/reported.
constraints: Do not rely on stateless one-shot Semantix calls for production Spec Studio sessions.
Decision IDs remain Phalanx-owned; Semantix may preserve or reference them but must not mint canonical audit decision IDs.
tasks.comment: Landed packages/stx/src/spec-studio-id-continuity.js: checkIdContinuity / assertIdContinuity compare prior vs next packet for requirements / findings / groundedFacts plus context-request reissue. Catches drops, silent same-id mutation, supersedion w/o replacement, reuse-after-supersede, resolved-finding regression, resolved silent-remove. Decisions stay Phalanx-owned. 21 tests pass.

tasks.title: Implement readiness classifier and update-safety rules
tasks.goal: Teach Semantix to classify ready, needs_user, and blocked for greenfield, update, unknown, duplicate, replacement, and degraded cases using the Spec Studio contract rules.
definition_of_done: Greenfield requests can be ready only when alignedRequirement, scope, must-level acceptance, and obvious exclusions are clear.
Update requests can be ready only with targetSurfaces plus doNotChange/reuse/compatibility boundaries.
Unknown new-vs-update classification returns needs_user with an open question and blocker finding.
Duplicate or replacement of an existing surface without explicit approval returns blocked or needs_user, never ready.
Negative requirements and do-not-change boundaries are first-class requirement facts.
constraints: Missing update target surface means needs_user, not ready.
Do not treat update requests as greenfield when current-system context is missing.
Semantix readiness is advisory; Phalanx remains lock authority.
tasks.comment: Landed packages/stx/src/spec-studio-readiness.js: classifyReadiness handles greenfield (alignedRequirement+scope+must-acceptance+exclusions), update (targetSurfaces + doNotChange/reuse/compatibility), unknown (needs_user + Q-MODE-001 + blocker), duplicate/replacement (blocked/needs_user/explicit). applyReadinessVerdict merges classifier output preserving user findings. promoteNegativeRequirements lifts negatives + doNotChange to first-class facts. docs/plans report added. 24 tests.

tasks.title: Generate Phalanx-brokered context requests
tasks.goal: Emit focused SemantixContextRequest objects when existing-system facts materially affect readiness, instead of inventing facts or querying Hoplon directly.
definition_of_done: Semantix can emit context requests for identify_target_surface, summarize_current_behavior, find_existing_flow, find_reusable_component, find_constraints, collect_hoplon_evidence, and inspect_reference_artifact.
Requests include stable id, sessionId, iteration, purpose, query, requestedFrom, constraints, and reason.
Requests use requestedFrom/allowedSources to ask Phalanx to broker Hoplon/repo/upload/trace access.
Tests prove no direct Semantix-to-Hoplon call path exists in this slice.
constraints: Semantix must not query Hoplon directly.
Do not make unavailable context a fabricated fact.
Only request context when it materially affects readiness or acceptance clarity.
tasks.comment: This is the Semantix half of the Phalanx-owned context broker boundary.

tasks.title: Ingest Phalanx context responses without mixing interpretation into facts
tasks.goal: Consume SemantixContextResponse facts/artifacts from Phalanx and update packets while preserving evidence refs and separating grounded facts from Semantix interpretation.
definition_of_done: Context response facts with evidence refs become groundedFacts with stable FACT IDs, source, text, confidence, and evidenceRef.
Unavailable/empty/error context responses become contextSources with status unavailable or skipped and do not fabricate groundedFacts.
Semantix interpretations from grounded facts go into assumptions, risks, findings, recommendations, or requirement facts, not groundedFacts.
Tests cover successful Hoplon-grounded fact, unavailable context, skipped context, and fact/interpretation separation.
constraints: Grounded facts require evidence refs.
Hoplon is context acquisition only, not spec authority.
User approval and locked artifact remain the desired-behavior authority.
tasks.comment: Pairs with the context-request generator; closes the evidence-ref and separation guarantees.

tasks.title: Implement user-turn, reconsider, skip, and decide-all semantics
tasks.goal: Support the interactive Spec Studio loop where user answers, free text, reconsiderations, skipped gaps, and delegated Semantix decisions trigger packet re-evaluation.
definition_of_done: Choice and free-text user turns update requirements, findings, decisions, openQuestions, coverage hints, and nextTurn deterministically enough for tests.
Reconsider supersedes prior decisions or requirements instead of rewriting history.
Skip or flag-as-gap preserves an auditable finding or concern.
decided-by-semantix decisions are flagged for human visibility and never treated as canonical Phalanx audit IDs.
constraints: Semantix may propose decision semantics, but Phalanx mints canonical decision IDs.
Findings cannot be silently dismissed.
Do not implement Phalanx persistence in Semantix.
tasks.comment: Makes the packet evolve across the actual Spec Studio conversation, not just initial prompt evaluation.

tasks.title: Produce honest degraded/unavailable Semantix packet behavior
tasks.goal: Ensure Semantix partial failure returns needs_user with blocker findings and never silently passes a degraded state into lock or Staff planning.
definition_of_done: Partial Semantix degraded packet uses source semantix, readiness needs_user, a blocker finding, alignmentPct 0 unless prior packet is explicitly stale-safe, and no new ungrounded requirement facts.
Malformed or unavailable model/evaluator output degrades honestly in the Semantix adapter surface.
Tests prove degraded packets validate but are not lockable by downstream contract expectations.
Docs/report state that fully unreachable Semantix fallback envelopes remain Phalanx-owned.
constraints: Do not mark degraded packets ready.
Do not start or modify Phalanx run behavior.
Do not invent facts during degradation.
tasks.comment: Landed packages/stx/src/spec-studio-degraded.js: createDegradedPacket (source=semantix, readiness=needs_user, blocker, alignmentPct=0 unless staleSafe carries prior coverage, no invented facts), isPacketLockable / isDegradedPacket predicates, withDegradationFallback wrapper that catches throws and malformed responses + demotes dishonest ready packets. docs/plans degraded-behavior boundary report added. 20 tests.

tasks.title: Guard against Semantix emitting Staff-owned planning content
tasks.goal: Prevent Semantix output from carrying Staff design docs, feature puzzles, verify commands, implementation plans, or file-change authority inside packets or next-turn bodies.
definition_of_done: Validator/evaluator rejects or reports Staff-owned fields at any packet depth, including nextTurn bodies and requirement extras.
Semantix can recommend clarification, assumptions, risks, findings, and acceptance criteria but not Staff implementation plans.
Tests cover top-level and nested Staff-owned output bleed.
Execution report documents the authority boundary.
constraints: Staff plans only after lock from the locked artifact.
Semantix aligns user intent; it does not execute code or choose verify commands.
Do not weaken packet passthrough so much that Staff-owned fields can hide in nested payloads.
tasks.comment: Landed packages/stx/src/spec-studio-no-staff-authority.js: deep-walking guard catches Staff-owned keys at any depth (requirements, findings, contextSources, groundedFacts, nextTurn.body, options). Case-insensitive fragment match handles mixed_case / snake_case. Free-text fields still allowed. STAFF_OWNED_FIELDS centralized; validateSemantixAlignmentPacket delegates. docs/plans authority-boundary report added. 23 tests.

tasks.title: Build upstream sample fixture and regression matrix
tasks.goal: Create compact Semantix-side fixtures and targeted tests for all sample packet flows and evaluator transitions required by the contract.
definition_of_done: Fixtures cover greenfield ready, update ready, ambiguous needs_user, replacement blocked, Hoplon-grounded update, degraded/unavailable, malformed context, and nested Staff-owned field bleed.
Targeted tests run without a live Phalanx or Hoplon service.
Fixtures stay compact and do not copy unrelated prose from the contract docs.
A report lists what samples were used and what remains out of scope.
constraints: Do not depend on a live Semantix service process in fixture tests.
Do not copy large doc sections into fixtures.
Do not use fixture success as proof of Phalanx lock authority.
tasks.comment: This gives Codex a compact review harness for each future Claude slice.

tasks.title: Expose a Phalanx capability-handshake adapter
tasks.goal: Provide a small callable Semantix adapter that Phalanx can use to verify Semantix packet generation, malformed-output handling, unavailable behavior, and prior-state preservation.
definition_of_done: Adapter can run in fixture/simulated mode from Phalanx tests without a long-running Semantix service.
Adapter returns packets that pass the Semantix-side and Phalanx-side contract validators.
Unavailable adapter behavior is explicit and degraded, not a thrown opaque failure.
Tests cover successful packet validation, malformed response, unavailable Semantix, and prior-state ID preservation.
constraints: Do not require editing Project-Phalanx from this task.
Do not make Semantix unavailable a terminal Phalanx pipeline failure.
Do not hardcode one machine-specific repo path as the only integration mode.
tasks.comment: This is the Semantix-side counterpart to Phalanx task rm-semantix-capability-handshake.

tasks.title: Prove Semantix-side Spec Studio evaluator flows end to end
tasks.goal: Run a focused Semantix-side proof for new, update, ambiguous, blocked replacement, degraded, Hoplon-grounded context-response, and multi-turn reconsider flows.
definition_of_done: A focused proof suite exercises the evaluator through greenfield ready, update ready, ambiguous needs_user, blocked replacement, degraded, Hoplon-grounded update, reconsider/supersede, and skipped-gap cases.
The proof asserts packet validity, stable IDs, fact/interpretation separation, no Staff-owned output, and correct readiness behavior.
The execution report states exactly which flows are Semantix-side only and which require Phalanx lock/session/handoff work.
Tracker task context records test commands and results for Codex review.
constraints: Do not claim Phalanx UI/session/lock/Staff integration is complete from Semantix-only proof.
Do not start pipeline runs automatically.
Use targeted tests and a concise execution report.
tasks.comment: Final Semantix-side proof before Codex review and Phalanx-side handshake integration.
